items.name: Interactive Simulations in Gemini
items.tagline: Gemini now lets you play with the concepts you ask about
items.description: Gemini can now transform complex topics into custom, interactive 3D visualizations directly within your chat. Instead of static text, get functional simulations you can manipulate in real-time. Just ask Gemini to “show me” or “help me visualize.”

items.name: Music Marketplace by Eleven Labs
items.tagline: Create a track. Publish it. Earn when it is used.
items.description: Music licensing is broken. Sync fees, clearance delays, per-use negotiations. ElevenCreative's Music Marketplace flips it: generate a track, publish it, earn every time it's downloaded or remixed. $11M paid to voice creators. Now music gets the same engine.

items.name: Ray
items.tagline: Your personal CFO in the terminal
items.description: Most personal finance apps show you charts. Ray tells you what to do and actually helps you plan. A terminal-based CFO that reads your real transactions, remembers your goals, and runs on your computer — not someone else's server. Open source. Free with your own keys.

items.name: R0Y
items.tagline: Natural language to Investing dashboards in seconds.
items.description: R0Y is a natural language financial studio that builds full investing dashboards in seconds. Ask for projections, macro analysis, backtests, or strategy models — it connects hundreds of millions of data points and generates live, interactive financial systems instantly. Collaborate with your team, share dashboards, and tailor everything to you or your organization. No code. No templates. Just intelligence on demand.

items.name: Edgee Codex Compressor
items.description: We benchmarked Codex alone against Codex routed through Edgee's compression gateway on the same repo, with the same model, under the same workflow. The result: Codex + Edgee used 49.5% fewer input tokens, improved cache hit rate from 76.1% to 85.4%, and reduced total session cost by 35.6%. This post breaks down why context compression makes Codex more efficient, more frugal, and materially cheaper to run without sacrificing useful output.

items.name: Layered
items.tagline: Turn your selfies into a personal AI stylist
items.description: Other AI stylists want you to photograph 100 items one by one. Layered reads your selfies instead and builds your closet for you. Get daily outfit picks from what you already own, Pinterest-style lookbooks from any new dress, and travel capsules packed for your destination, weather, and luggage size. Need more? Track cost-per-wear, spot clothes you never wear, plan cleanups, and get tomorrow's outfit on a Home screen widget.

items.name: ClarifierAI for IOS
items.tagline: Use AI for writing & translating your messages 10x faster
items.description: ClarifierAI keyboard for iPhone makes your words clearer and translates across 113 languages in any app. Available on iOS. Just a keyboard that activates when you need it. Works in Gmail, LinkedIn, iMessage, WhatsApp, Slack and more ⚡️

items.name: Nicelydone MCP
items.description: AI-generated pages all look the same. Nicelydone MCP gives your agent access to 140,000+ real screens, user flows, and UI components from shipped products. Works with Claude, Cursor, VS Code, and more.

items.name: LLM-Citeops
items.tagline: Fix 60% scores of AEO and GEO scores without single penny.
items.description: Free CLI for Node.js. Score pages for answer-ready content (AEO) and AI trust (GEO). Print HTML, JSON, or CSV. Fail CI when scores slip.

items.name: ARCH
items.tagline: Design production‑grade cloud architecture in your browser
items.description: ARCH is a local-first architecture designer for cloud and on-prem diagrams. Drag real provider components (AWS, Azure, GCP, IBM, Oracle, Alibaba, Huawei, on-prem), connect them with readable edges, nest regions/VPCs/subnets, and export presentation-ready PNG, SVG, or PDF and arch import/export. Visualize turns JSON, YAML, Terraform/HCL, and more into charts and explorer views. No account, no server: your work stays in the browser (IndexedDB), with offline-friendly loading after the first visit.
